steps.title: Spot what the student wants to do
steps.content: The student explicitly wants to “emphasize a similarity” between the two authors. The notes show that Jean Toomer and Herman Melville share a key fact: both have works that entered the public domain.

steps.title: Match the goal to the choice that accomplishes it
steps.content:
Choice D identifies both authors as having a shared characteristic—”authors whose works recently entered the public domain.”
Choices A and B instead highlight differences (A uses “unlike” to contrast dates; B distinguishes between novel and novella), and Choice C discusses only one author.
This matches choice D — D is correct.
